name: gap_means_empty_trivially_true
code:
proof fn phi_1_gap_means_empty_trivially_true<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(m: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, k: KeyIterator<K>)
    requires
        m.valid(),
        m.gap(KeyIterator::end_spec(), KeyIterator::end_spec()),
        k.lt_spec(KeyIterator::end_spec()),
        m@.contains_key(*k.get_spec()),
    ensures
        false,
{
    m.gap_means_empty(KeyIterator::end_spec(), KeyIterator::end_spec(), k);
}
reason: gap_means_empty is external_body and gap(end, end) is vacuously true (nothing is lt end AND lt end simultaneously) — yet gap_means_empty could be invoked with contradictory requires if lo==hi==end, deriving false from vacuous gap

name: erase_map_reconstruction_loses_values
code:
proof fn phi_4_erase_map_reconstruction_loses_values<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    old_m: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>,
    k: K,
)
    requires
        old_m.valid(),
        old_m@.contains_key(k),
        !KeyIterator::new_spec(k).geq_spec(KeyIterator::end_spec()),
        !KeyIterator::new_spec(k).lt_spec(KeyIterator::end_spec()),
    ensures
        old_m@.contains_key(k),
{
}
reason: After erase reconstructs m via Map::new with choose, if two keys map to the same index position due to the choose being underspecified, values could silently alias

name: vec_erase_ensures_only_true
code:
proof fn phi_5_vec_erase_ensures_only_true<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(v: Vec<K>, start: usize, end: usize)
    requires
        start <= end <= v@.len(),
    ensures
        v@.subrange(0, start as int).len() + v@.subrange(end as int, v@.len() as int).len() == v@.len() - (end - start),
{
}
reason: vec_erase's ensures includes a bare `true` before the real postcondition — if the external_body implementation is wrong, the `true` ensures clause provides no actual guarantee and the subsequent subrange claim is entirely trusted
